quizQuestion:
Espero que {___} pronto.
(I hope you arrive soon.)
sentenceTranslation: I hope you arrive soon.
explanation: llegues — present subjunctive of llegar (tú). After espero que, use the subjunctive. -ar subjunctive pattern: hablar → hable, hables, hable... Note: llegar → llegue (not *llegues for yo/él forms).

quizQuestion:
Espera hasta que {___} Juan.
(Wait until Juan arrives.)
sentenceTranslation: Wait until Juan arrives.
explanation: llegue — present subjunctive of llegar (él/ella/usted). Hasta que requires subjunctive when referring to a future event. Same form as yo: llegue.

quizQuestion:
Quiero que {___} con él.
(I want you to talk to him.)
sentenceTranslation: I want you to talk to him.
explanation: hables — present subjunctive of hablar (tú). Quiero que requires subjunctive in the subordinate clause. Pattern: hablar → hable, hables, hable, hablemos, habléis, hablen.

quizQuestion:
Es importante que {___} claramente.
(It's important that we speak clearly.)
sentenceTranslation: It's important that we speak clearly.
explanation: hablemos — present subjunctive of hablar (nosotros). Impersonal es importante que triggers subjunctive. Nosotros form always ends in -emos for -ar verbs.

quizQuestion:
No quiero que {___} tan alto.
(I don't want them to speak so loudly.)
sentenceTranslation: I don't want them to speak so loudly.
explanation: hablen — present subjunctive of hablar (ellos/ellas/ustedes). After no quiero que, the subjunctive is required. Third person plural: hablen.

quizQuestion:
Es necesario que {___} más.
(It's necessary that you work more.)
sentenceTranslation: It's necessary that you work more.
explanation: trabajes — present subjunctive of trabajar (tú). Es necesario que triggers subjunctive. Regular -ar pattern: trabajar → trabaje, trabajes, trabaje.

quizQuestion:
Prefiero que {___} en silencio.
(I prefer that he work in silence.)
sentenceTranslation: I prefer that he work in silence.
explanation: trabaje — present subjunctive of trabajar (él/ella/usted). Prefiero que requires subjunctive. Note: same form for yo and él/ella in the present subjunctive.

quizQuestion:
Espero que no {___} nada malo.
(I hope nothing bad happens.)
sentenceTranslation: I hope nothing bad happens.
explanation: pase — present subjunctive of pasar (él/ella/ello). After espero que, subjunctive required. Regular -ar: pasar → pase, pases, pase.

quizQuestion:
Cuando {___}, estaré en casa.
(When you call, I'll be at home.)
sentenceTranslation: When you call, I'll be at home.
explanation: llames — present subjunctive of llamar (tú). Cuando + subjunctive is used when referring to a future event. Compare: Cuando llamas siempre estoy durmiendo (present indicative for habitual past action).

quizQuestion:
Quiero que {___} más verduras.
(I want you to eat more vegetables.)
sentenceTranslation: I want you to eat more vegetables.
explanation: comas — present subjunctive of comer (tú). Quiero que requires subjunctive. -er subjunctive pattern: comer → coma, comas, coma, comamos, comáis, coman (opposite vowel: -ar verbs use -e, -er/-ir verbs use -a).

quizQuestion:
Es bueno que {___} fruta cada día.
(It's good that he eats fruit every day.)
sentenceTranslation: It's good that he eats fruit every day.
explanation: coma — present subjunctive of comer (él/ella/usted/yo). Es bueno que triggers subjunctive. Note: yo and él/ella/usted have the same form in the present subjunctive.

quizQuestion:
No quiero que {___} tanto café.
(I don't want you to drink so much coffee.)
sentenceTranslation: I don't want you to drink so much coffee.
explanation: bebas — present subjunctive of beber (tú). No quiero que triggers subjunctive. -er pattern: beber → beba, bebas, beba, bebamos.

quizQuestion:
Espero que {___} mucho en este curso.
(I hope you learn a lot in this course.)
sentenceTranslation: I hope you learn a lot in this course.
explanation: aprendas — present subjunctive of aprender (tú). After espero que, subjunctive required. aprender → aprenda, aprendas, aprenda, aprendamos.

quizQuestion:
Quiero que {___} cerca de mí.
(I want you to live close to me.)
sentenceTranslation: I want you to live close to me.
explanation: vivas — present subjunctive of vivir (tú). Quiero que requires subjunctive. -ir pattern: vivir → viva, vivas, viva, vivamos, viváis, vivan.

quizQuestion:
Es posible que {___} en España.
(It's possible that he lives in Spain.)
sentenceTranslation: It's possible that he lives in Spain.
explanation: viva — present subjunctive of vivir (él/ella/yo). Es posible que triggers subjunctive. Note: viva is the same form for both yo and él/ella/usted.

quizQuestion:
Te pido que {___} la respuesta.
(I ask you to write the answer.)
sentenceTranslation: I ask you to write the answer.
explanation: escribas — present subjunctive of escribir (tú). Te pido que requires subjunctive. escribir → escriba, escribas, escriba.

quizQuestion:
{___} haga buen tiempo mañana.
(I hope the weather is good tomorrow.)
sentenceTranslation: I hope the weather is good tomorrow.
explanation: ojalá — from Arabic 'inshallah' (if God wills). Ojalá always requires the subjunctive. It expresses a wish or hope. Ojalá + present subjunctive = I hope... (possible wish).

quizQuestion:
Te lo explico {___} lo entiendas.
(I'll explain it so that you understand.)
sentenceTranslation: I'll explain it so that you understand.
explanation: para que — used to express purpose. Para que ALWAYS requires the subjunctive. Contrast with para + infinitive (same subject): estudio para aprender vs. te explico para que aprendas (different subjects).

quizQuestion:
{___} llegues, llámame.
(When you arrive, call me.)
sentenceTranslation: When you arrive, call me.
explanation: cuando — when referring to a future event, cuando requires the subjunctive. Compare: Cuando llegué (past — indicative preterite) vs. Cuando llegues (future — subjunctive). This distinction is a classic B1 trap.

quizQuestion:
Llámame {___} salgas.
(Call me before you leave.)
sentenceTranslation: Call me before you leave.
explanation: antes de que — always requires the subjunctive. Compare with antes de + infinitive (same subject): Me ducho antes de salir. vs. Llámame antes de que salgas (different subjects → antes de que + subjunctive).

quizQuestion:
No saldré {___} termines.
(I won't leave until you finish.)
sentenceTranslation: I won't leave until you finish.
explanation: hasta que — requires subjunctive when referring to a future/hypothetical event. No saldré hasta que termines = I won't leave until you finish (future event). Compare: Esperé hasta que terminó (past — indicative).

quizQuestion:
{___} estés aquí.
(I'm glad you're here.)
sentenceTranslation: I'm glad you're here.
explanation: me alegro de que — expressions of emotion always require the subjunctive when there are two different subjects. Me alegro de que + subjunctive = I'm glad that... Other emotion triggers: siento que, me gusta que, tengo miedo de que.

quizQuestion:
{___} sea verdad.
(I don't think it's true.)
sentenceTranslation: I don't think it's true.
explanation: no creo que — expressions of doubt/denial require the subjunctive. Creo que + indicative (belief), but no creo que + subjunctive (doubt). Critical B1 contrast: Creo que es verdad vs. No creo que sea verdad.

quizQuestion:
{___} estudies cada día.
(It's important that you study every day.)
sentenceTranslation: It's important that you study every day.
explanation: es importante que — impersonal expressions of obligation/value always require the subjunctive. Common B1 triggers: es importante que, es necesario que, es posible que, es mejor que, es bueno que, es una pena que.

quizQuestion:
{___} llueva mañana.
(It's possible that it will rain tomorrow.)
sentenceTranslation: It's possible that it will rain tomorrow.
explanation: es posible que — possibility expressions require the subjunctive. Compare: quizás/tal vez + subjunctive (same idea). Contrast: es seguro que + indicative (certainty triggers indicative).

quizQuestion:
{___} venga hoy.
(I doubt he'll come today.)
sentenceTranslation: I doubt he'll come today.
explanation: dudo que — verbs of doubt always require subjunctive. dudo que = I doubt that. Other doubt triggers: no pienso que, no creo que, no es verdad que, es imposible que.

quizQuestion:
{___} tengas suerte.
(I hope you have luck.)
sentenceTranslation: I hope you have luck.
explanation: espero que — one of the most common B1 subjunctive triggers. Espero que is a verb of volition (wanting/hoping). Always followed by subjunctive. esperar que = to hope that / to expect that.

quizQuestion:
{___} vengas conmigo.
(I want you to come with me.)
sentenceTranslation: I want you to come with me.
explanation: quiero que — the most iconic B1 subjunctive trigger. Verbs of wanting/wishing require subjunctive when the subjects differ: Quiero IR (same subject — infinitive) vs. Quiero que VENGAS (different subjects — subjunctive).
